mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases over a 5-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of periods following INDPRO acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 65% of observations when both inflation and rate thresholds are breached.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows or positive returns drop below 45% when PAYEMS expands.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals weakening aggregate demand and recessionary pressures, prompting equity market sell-offs that yield negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust economic activity and capacity utilization, generating immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in greater than 55% of historical occurrences.
